description: Behavioural evaluation set for the oneshot skill. Cases pin the Explore→Code→Test workflow, the complexity-check circuit breaker (escalation to /apex or /spec), and the disciplined output format.

evals.name: single-file-trivial-edit
evals.prompt: Add a console.log of the user id at the top of the login function in src/auth/login.ts.
evals.expected_output: Single-file edit. Workflow: brief Glob/Grep to locate the function, Edit to insert the log, run lint + typecheck. Output report follows the documented format: ## Done block with Task, Files changed, Validation lines.

evals.name: resolves-github-issue-reference
evals.expected_output: Resolves the issue via `gh issue view 42 --json title,body,labels`. Uses the issue title + body as task description. If the body has acceptance criteria, treats them as the implementation checklist.

evals.name: circuit-breaker-fires-on-multi-file
evals.prompt: Rewrite the authentication system to support OAuth, magic links, and SAML SSO across the API, the web app, and the mobile app.
evals.expected_output: After Explore, the complexity check fires (>5 files, >2 distinct systems, cross-cutting). Skill stops, warns the user with a concrete reason, and recommends `/apex <task>` or `/spec -s <task>` instead. Waits for explicit confirmation before continuing.

evals.name: circuit-breaker-fires-on-cross-cutting
evals.prompt: Add a new column to the users table — store last_active timestamp and update the auth flow + admin dashboard to display it.
evals.expected_output: Complexity check fires on cross-cutting concerns (database migration + multi-component change). Recommends `/spec` to plan the migration steps and rollback strategy.

evals.name: stays-in-scope
evals.prompt: Fix the typo in the welcome email template (welcome.txt).
evals.expected_output: Single-file Edit, no tangential improvements. Does NOT refactor the surrounding template, does NOT add comments, does NOT improve unrelated style. Output report shows exactly one file changed.

evals.name: stops-after-two-attempts
evals.prompt: Update the deployment script to use the new Cloudflare Workers API. (The script has unfamiliar legacy patterns.)
evals.expected_output: If after 2 attempts the change still fails (lint, typecheck, or behaviour), the skill reports the blocker and stops. Does not thrash with a third attempt. Output cites the specific failure mode and suggests a heavier-weight skill (`/apex` or `/spec`) for next iteration.
